title: Implement Request and Full Access permission model with minimal connector tool exposure
summary: Completed through direct edits and reconciliation on main. Request / Full Access repository policy, controllerHome persistence, Work permission snapshots, minimal core exposure with always-available access controls, GUI selector, high-risk safeguards, runtime cleanup, compatibility validation, and final GUI script fixes are integrated and pushed at 635d476. Historical T1 remains cancelled because the optional agent run failed before producing changes; T2 is the authoritative completed replacement.
goals: Default connector tools/list exposes only the small facade surface required for normal ChatGPT operation.
Request and Full Access modes are persisted under controllerHome.
Each Work captures an immutable effective permission snapshot at start.
Controller GUI shows the active mode, effective state, and allows safe switching.
High-risk, destructive, remote-write, and secret-sensitive actions retain explicit confirmation/approval fallback regardless of mode.
Add focused tests and documentation without broad test expansion.
Commit changes on an isolated feature branch, merge into main safely, delete the feature branch, and clean its worktree.
nonGoals: Do not remove internal advanced tools from the server implementation; restrict connector exposure/discovery instead.
Do not weaken existing destructive, remote-write, secret, or policy gates.
Do not rewrite unrelated GUI/runtime architecture.
Do not modify or discard pre-existing dirty main working-tree changes.
acceptanceCriteria: A fresh connector tools/list contains the intended minimal facade set by default, with an explicit advanced/full-access mechanism rather than the full legacy surface.
Request and Full Access settings survive controller restart via controllerHome storage.
Started Work records include the resolved mode and capability/approval snapshot used for execution.
GUI displays and switches access mode with clear pending/effective state.
Existing high-risk approval behavior remains covered by focused tests.
Targeted type/tests pass and diff is reviewed.
Feature commit is merged into main without disturbing pre-existing main working-tree changes; feature branch/worktree are removed.

tasks.title: Implement permission model and minimal connector exposure
tasks.objective: Inspect the MCP tool registration/list path, controllerHome runtime configuration, Work creation, policy gates, and local controller GUI. Implement Request / Full Access persistence and snapshots, default minimal tool exposure, GUI switching/status, compatibility and focused tests/docs. Work only in an isolated worktree and preserve existing high-risk approvals.
acceptanceCriteria: Default tools/list is minimal while internal advanced capabilities remain available behind the designed access path.
Request/Full Access persists in controllerHome and is represented in Work snapshots.
GUI can switch and display mode/effective permissions.

commandEvidence.stdout: Compatibility surface passed with 2 access tools, 11 core tools, 76 advanced tools, and 234 full tools.
Controller V8 verification passed, including generated dashboard JavaScript parsing.
46 focused tests passed across access policy, access MCP tools, GUI mode selection, runtime cleanup, core tool exposure, and restart ownership.

acceptanceResults.criterion: Minimal core tool exposure includes access-mode controls.
acceptanceResults.evidence: Core exposes 11 bounded tools including repository_access_get/set; compatibility and exposure tests pass.

acceptanceResults.evidence: Access policy and access-aware work routing tests pass; policy path is controllerHome repository storage.

acceptanceResults.criterion: GUI can select and display access mode.
acceptanceResults.evidence: Local Bridge access-mode tests pass and the complete generated dashboard script parses successfully.

acceptanceResults.criterion: High-risk, remote-write, destructive, and secret gates remain protected.
acceptanceResults.evidence: Access decision matrix confirms Full Access is repository-local only; remote/destructive still require approval and secrets remain denied.
